reasoning: The ensures restates preconditions, but the preconditions are simultaneously satisfiable — neither `container_perms_wf` nor `container_tree_wf` places any constraint on `owned_endpoints`. The code comments explicitly describe unique ownership intent ("each endpoint can ONLY be referenced by threads in the subtree"), yet no spec enforces endpoint disjointness.

The spec never relates `scheduler` contents to `owned_threads`. A container's scheduler can contain threads not in its `owned_threads` set, breaking the containment model where only owned threads should be schedulable.

This is a desirable property. `container_subtree_set_wf` requires `sub_c_ptr`'s depth strictly greater than `c_ptr`'s depth. Since a container cannot have depth strictly greater than itself, no container can be in its own subtree — this is the standard definition of a proper subtree (excluding self) and prevents cycles.

The ensures `is_None() || is_Some()` is a tautology (law of excluded middle) — it verifies trivially regardless of spec content. While the observation about root's parent being unconstrained may have merit, this PHI fails to demonstrate it since the ensures proves nothing.
